facts.label: SMALLEST NATION TO EVER QUALIFY
facts.story: With a population of just 1.3 million, Trinidad and Tobago became the smallest nation ever to reach a World Cup finals when they beat Bahrain in a playoff to book their spot at Germany 2006 — a Dennis Lawrence header in Manama sealing it. The record stood as the smallest population to qualify since Northern Ireland in 1958, until Iceland, with even fewer people, broke it in 2018.

facts.label: TEN MEN AGAINST THE WORLD
facts.story: Less than 30 seconds into the second half of their first-ever World Cup match, defender Avery John was sent off against Sweden, leaving Trinidad and Tobago to defend for 45 minutes a man down. They somehow held firm for a scoreless draw in Dortmund, with 34-year-old captain Dwight Yorke — a Manchester United treble winner — earning Man of the Match honours in his country's greatest football moment.

annotations.label: A HISTORIC CLEAN SHEET
annotations.story: Down to ten men within seconds of the restart after Avery John's red card, Trinidad and Tobago somehow shut out a talented Swedish side 0–0 in Dortmund. Captain Dwight Yorke marshaled the defense and was named Man of the Match in the country's first-ever World Cup appearance.

annotations.label: LATE GOALS SINK THE WARRIORS
annotations.story: Trinidad and Tobago held England scoreless deep into the second half in Nuremberg before Peter Crouch's header and a deflected Steven Gerrard strike in the final ten minutes secured a 2–0 win for the eventual quarter-finalists, ending the Soca Warriors' dream of a shock result.

annotations.label: A QUIET GOODBYE
annotations.story: An early own goal from Brent Sancho put Trinidad and Tobago behind against Paraguay, and Nelson Cuevas added a late second in a 2–0 defeat. It confirmed a group-stage exit and made Trinidad and Tobago the only team at the entire 2006 World Cup not to score a single goal.
